mission.title: Install and Configure Vineyard on Kubernetes
mission.description: Vineyard is an in-memory immutable data manager designed for efficient data sharing among big data systems. Installing Vineyard allows you to leverage its capabilities for high-level abstractions and zero-copy data sharing in various data-intensive tasks.

steps.title: Add the Vineyard Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Vineyard Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add vineyard https://vineyard.oss-ap-southeast-1.aliyuncs.com/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the Vineyard Operator
steps.description:
Install the Vineyard Operator using Helm with the specified version and create a namespace if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install vineyard-operator vineyard/vineyard-operator --version 0.24.4 --namespace vineyard --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Vineyard Operator pod is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace vineyard
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the resource limits for the Vineyard Operator to ensure optimal performance. You can edit the Helm release with:
```bash
helm upgrade vineyard-operator vineyard/vineyard-operator --namespace vineyard --set controllerManager.manager.resources.limits.cpu=500m --set controllerManager.manager.resources.limits.memory=500Mi
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring (optional)
steps.description:
If you want to enable metrics for monitoring, you can expose the metrics service by running:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/vineyard-metrics --namespace vineyard 8443:8443
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Vineyard Operator pod running in the 'vineyard' namespace. You can also verify that the service is accessible for monitoring if you set it up.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add vineyard https://vineyard.oss-ap-southeast-1.aliyuncs.com/charts
helm repo update

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Vineyard Operator Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster. Use the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall vineyard-operator --namespace vineyard
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Vineyard. Run the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd vineyards.vineyard.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace vineyard
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the Vineyard Operator and verify that it has been removed. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace vineyard
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Vineyard installation. You can export the current configuration with:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace vineyard -o yaml > vineyard-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Vineyard Operator to the latest version. Execute the following commands:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade vineyard-operator vineyard/vineyard-operator --version 0.25.0 --namespace vineyard
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Vineyard Operator pod is running with the new version after the upgrade. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace vineyard
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If your Vineyard Operator pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors. Use the following command to view the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace vineyard
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Vineyard service, ensure that the service is running and check the endpoints. Use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace vineyard
kubectl describe svc vineyard-metrics --namespace vineyard
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the Vineyard Operator fails to start due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits. You can view the current configuration with:
```bash
kubectl get deployment vineyard-operator --namespace vineyard -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: CRDs not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues related to missing CRDs, verify that the CRDs are installed correctly. Check the CRDs with:
```bash
kubectl get crd --namespace vineyard
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm CLI installed to deploy Vineyard.
